taxonomy: Pigea latifolia is a genus of flowering plants in the family Sapindaceae. The Sapindaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the soapberry family. This family contains approximately 130 genera and 1,200 species. Pigea latifolia is native to parts of Asia and its classification places it within the Sapindaceae family. The classification of Pigea latifolia is still being researched, but it is currently grouped under this family.
properties: Pigea latifolia, also known as the broad-leaved pigeonwings or Mexican pigeonwings, has been found to possess various therapeutic properties. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Studies have shown that extracts from Pigea latifolia may exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities, which could be beneficial in treating various diseases. Additionally, the plant is reported to have wound-healing properties, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Pigea latifolia.
constituents: Pigea latifolia contains active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These compounds are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids present in Pigea latifolia, including kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to have potential health benefits. The plant's phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, may also have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The terpenoids in Pigea latifolia have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
parts: Pigea latifolia. The medicinal parts of Pigea latifolia, commonly known as Pigea or Pigea latifolia, are its roots and leaves. These parts are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions. The roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, while the leaves are used to treat fever and other respiratory issues.
